A Network Connection connects the Client to the Server
A Network Connection provides the means to send an ordered, lossless, stream of bytes in both directions
When an Application Message is transported by MQTT it contains payload data, a Quality of Service (QoS), a collection of Properties, and a Topic Name
A Client opens the Network Connection to the Server
A Server accepts Network Connections from Clients
A Server accepts Application Messages published by Clients
A Server processes Subscribe and Unsubscribe requests from Clients
A Server forwards Application Messages that match Client Subscriptions
A Server closes the Network Connection from the Client
A Subscription comprises a Topic Filter and a maximum QoS
A Subscription is associated with a single Session
A Session can contain more than one Subscription
Each Subscription within a Session has a different Topic Filter
A Shared Subscription comprises a Topic Filter and a maximum QoS
A Shared Subscription can be associated with more than one Session
An Application Message that matches a Shared Subscription is only sent to the Client associated with one of these Sessions
A Session can subscribe to more than one Shared Subscription
A Session can contain both Shared Subscriptions and Subscriptions which are not shared
A Wildcard Subscription is a Subscription with a Topic Filter containing one or more wildcard characters
The Wildcard Subscription allows the subscription to match more than one Topic Name
The label attached to an Application Message which is matched against the Subscriptions known to the Server
An expression contained in a Subscription to indicate an interest in one or more topics
A Topic Filter can include wildcard characters
A control packet that cannot be parsed according to this specification
An error that is detected after the packet has been parsed and found to contain data that is not allowed by the protocol or is inconsistent with the state of the Client or Server
An Application Message which is published by the Server after the Network Connection is closed in cases where the Network Connection is not closed normally
The set of Unicode Control Codes and Unicode Noncharacters which should not be included in a UTF-8 Encoded String
Prohibited: should not be included in a UTF-8 Encoded String

Prohibited: It is a Protocol Error to include the Subscription Identifier Available more than once
It is a Protocol Error to include the Subscription Identifier Available... or to send a value other than 0 or 1

3 MQTT Control Packets -> 3.10 UNSUBSCRI.data:
{
    "MUST be compared character-by-character": "The Topic Filters (whether they contain wildcards or not) supplied in an UNSUBSCRIBE packet MUST be compared character-by-character with the current set of Topic Filters held by the Server for the Client.",
    "MUST be deleted": "If any filter matches exactly then its owning Subscription MUST be deleted [MQTT-3.10.4-1]",
    "MUST stop adding any new messages": "When a Server receives an UNSUBSCRIBE packet, it MUST stop adding any new messages which match the Topic Filters for delivery to the Client [MQTT-3.10.4-2]",
    "MUST complete the delivery": "The Server MUST complete the delivery of any QoS 1 or QoS 2 messages which match the Topic Filters and that the Server has started to send to the Client [MQTT-3.10.4-3]",
    "MUST respond": "The Server MUST respond to an UNSUBSCRIBE request by sending an UNSUBACK packet [MQTT-3.10.4-4]",
    "MUST have the same Packet Identifier": "The UNSUBACK packet MUST have the same Packet Identifier as the UNSUBSCRIBE packet",
    "MUST respond with an UNSUBACK": "Even where no Topic Subscriptions are deleted, the Server MUST respond with an UNSUBACK [MQTT-3.10.4-5]",
    "MUST process that packet as if it had received a sequence of multiple UNSUBSCRIBE packets": "If a Server receives an UNSUBSCRIBE packet that contains multiple Topic Filters, it MUST process that packet as if it had received a sequence of multiple UNSUBSCRIBE packets, except that it sends just one UNSUBACK response [MQTT-3.10.4-6]"
}

MUST handle that packet as if it had received a sequence of multiple SUBSCRIBE packets
MUST contain a Reason Code for each Topic Filter/Subscription Option pair
MUST either show the maximum QoS that was granted for that Subscription or indicate that the subscription failed
MUST be the minimum of the QoS of the originally published message and the Maximum QoS granted by the Server
is not allowed to revert to the old identifier after it has sent a PUBLISH packet containing the new one
MUST NOT: MUST NOT be lost
is not allowed to revert to the old identifier

MUST: MUST assign an unused Packet Identifier each time the sender has a new Application Message to publish
MUST send a PUBLISH packet containing this Packet Identifier with QoS 1 and DUP flag set to 0
MUST treat the PUBLISH packet as unacknowledged until the sender has received the corresponding PUBACK packet from the receiver
MUST respond with a PUBACK packet containing the Packet Identifier from the incoming PUBLISH packet, having accepted ownership of the Application Message
MUST treat any incoming PUBLISH packet that contains the same Packet Identifier as being a new Application Message, irrespective of the setting of the DUP flag

Mandatory: MUST set the Authentication Method to the same value as the Authentication Method originally used to authenticate the Network Connection [MQTT-4.12.1-1]
MUST close the Network Connection [MQTT-4.12.1-2]
Recommended: SHOULD send DISCONNECT with an appropriate Reason Code as described in section 4.13

    "need to": "In order to implement QoS 1 and QoS 2 protocol flows, the Client and Server need to associate state with the Client Identifier.",
    "can continue": "The session can continue across a sequence of Network Connections.",
    "lasts": "The session lasts as long as the latest Network Connection plus the Session Expiry Interval.",
    "consists of": "The Session State in the Client consists of: QoS 1 and QoS 2 messages which have been sent to the Server, but have not been completely acknowledged. QoS 2 messages which have been received from the Server, but have not been completely acknowledged.",
    "consists of": "The Session State in the Server consists of: The existence of a Session, even if the rest of the Session State is empty. The Clients subscriptions, including any Subscription Identifiers. QoS 1 and QoS 2 messages which have been sent to the Client, but have not been completely acknowledged. QoS 1 and QoS 2 messages pending transmission to the Client and OPTIONALLY QoS 0 messages pending transmission to the Client. QoS 2 messages which have been received from the Client, but have not been completely acknowledged. The Will Message and the Will Delay Interval.",

Mandatory: conforms to this specification only if
satisfies all the statements below
matches the format described
satisfies the MUST level requirements
does not require the use of any extensions defined outside of the specification

MUST: MUST assign an unused Packet Identifier when the sender has a new Application Message to publish
MUST send a PUBLISH packet containing the assigned Packet Identifier with QoS 2 and DUP flag set to 0
MUST treat the PUBLISH packet as unacknowledged until the sender has received the corresponding PUBREC packet from the receiver
MUST send a PUBREL packet when the sender receives a PUBREC packet from the receiver with a Reason Code value less than 0x80
MUST contain the same Packet Identifier as the original PUBLISH packet
MUST treat the PUBREL packet as unacknowledged until the sender has received the corresponding PUBCOMP packet from the receiver
MUST respond with a PUBREC containing the Packet Identifier from the incoming PUBLISH packet, having accepted ownership of the Application Message
MUST treat any subsequent PUBLISH packet that contains that Packet Identifier as being a new Application Message
MUST acknowledge any subsequent PUBLISH packet with the same Packet Identifier by sending a PUBREC
MUST NOT cause duplicate messages to be delivered to any onward recipients in this case
MUST respond to a PUBREL packet by sending a PUBCOMP packet containing the same Packet Identifier as the PUBREL
MUST treat any subsequent PUBLISH packet that contains that Packet Identifier as being a new Application Message
MUST continue the QoS 2 acknowledgement sequence even if the receiver has applied message expiry
MUST NOT: MUST NOT re-send the PUBLISH once the sender has sent the corresponding PUBREL packet
MUST NOT apply Message expiry if a PUBLISH packet has been sent
MUST NOT cause duplicate messages to be delivered to any onward recipients in this case

MUST: MQTT Control Packets MUST be sent in WebSocket binary data frames
the recipient MUST close the Network Connection
The receiver MUST NOT assume that MQTT Control Packets are aligned on WebSocket frame boundaries
The Client MUST include mqtt in the list of WebSocket Sub Protocols it offers
The WebSocket Subprotocol name selected and returned by the Server MUST be mqtt
MUST NOT: the recipient MUST close the Network Connection
The receiver MUST NOT assume that MQTT Control Packets are aligned on WebSocket frame boundaries

MUST: MUST respond with the packet as determined by the QoS in the PUBLISH Packet [MQTT-3.3.4-1]
MUST deliver the message to the Client respecting the maximum QoS of all the matching subscriptions [MQTT-3.3.4-2]
MUST send those Subscription Identifiers in the message which is published as the result of the subscriptions [MQTT-3.3.4-3]
MUST include in the PUBLISH packet the Subscription Identifiers for all matching subscriptions which have a Subscription Identifier, their order is not significant [MQTT-3.3.4-4]
MUST send, in each of them, the Subscription Identifier of the matching subscription if it has a Subscription Identifier [MQTT-3.3.4-5]
MUST NOT contain a Subscription Identifier [MQTT-3.3.4-6]
MUST NOT send more than Receive Maximum QoS 1 and QoS 2 PUBLISH packets for which it has not received PUBACK, PUBCOMP, or PUBREC with a Reason Code of 128 or greater from the Server [MQTT-3.3.4-7]
MUST NOT delay the sending of any packets other than PUBLISH packets due to having sent Receive Maximum PUBLISH packets without receiving acknowledgements for them [MQTT-3.3.4-8]
MUST NOT send more than Receive Maximum QoS 1 and QoS 2 PUBLISH packets for which it has not received PUBACK, PUBCOMP, or PUBREC with a Reason Code of 128 or greater from the Client [MQTT-3.3.4-9]
MUST NOT delay the sending of any packets other than PUBLISH packets due to having sent Receive Maximum PUBLISH packets without receiving acknowledgements for them [MQTT-3.3.4-10]
MUST NOT: MUST NOT contain a Subscription Identifier [MQTT-3.3.4-6]
MUST NOT send more than Receive Maximum QoS 1 and QoS 2 PUBLISH packets for which it has not received PUBACK, PUBCOMP, or PUBREC with a Reason Code of 128 or greater from the Server [MQTT-3.3.4-7]
MUST NOT delay the sending of any packets other than PUBLISH packets due to having sent Receive Maximum PUBLISH packets without receiving acknowledgements for them [MQTT-3.3.4-8]
MUST NOT send more than Receive Maximum QoS 1 and QoS 2 PUBLISH packets for which it has not received PUBACK, PUBCOMP, or PUBREC with a Reason Code of 128 or greater from the Client [MQTT-3.3.4-9]
MUST NOT delay the sending of any packets other than PUBLISH packets due to having sent Receive Maximum PUBLISH packets without receiving acknowledgements for them [MQTT-3.3.4-10]
TECHNICAL_PROHIBITIONS: is a Protocol Error for a PUBLISH packet to contain any Subscription Identifier other than those received in a SUBSCRIBE packet which caused the PUBLISH packet to flow
A Topic Alias value of 0 or greater than the Maximum Topic Alias is a Protocol Error
If the packet has a zero length Topic Name field it is a Protocol Error
CONDITIONAL_CONSTRAINTS: If the Client receives an unsolicited Application Message (not resulting from a subscription) which has a QoS greater than Maximum QoS, the Client uses a DISCONNECT packet with Reason Code 0x9B (QoS not supported)

If the Server receives more than Receive Maximum QoS 1 and QoS 2 PUBLISH packets where it has not sent a PUBACK or PUBCOMP in response, the Server uses a DISCONNECT packet with Reason Code 0x93 (Receive Maximum exceeded)

If the Client receives more than Receive Maximum QoS 1 and QoS 2 PUBLISH packets where it has not sent a PUBACK or PUBCOMP in response, the Client uses DISCONNECT with Reason Code 0x93 (Receive Maximum exceeded)

mandatory: A Shared Subscription's Topic Filter MUST start with share/ and MUST contain a ShareName that is at least one character long [MQTT-4.8.2-1]
The ShareName MUST NOT contain the characters "/", "+" or "#", but MUST be followed by a "/" character
The "/" character after the ShareName MUST be followed by a Topic Filter [MQTT-4.8.2-2] as described in section 4.7
When sending an Application Message to a Client, the Server MUST respect the granted QoS for the Client's subscription [MQTT-4.8.2-3]

If the Server is in the process of sending a QoS 2 message to its chosen subscribing Client and the connection to the Client breaks before delivery is complete, the Server MUST complete the delivery of the message to that Client when it reconnects [MQTT-4.8.2-4]
If the Client's Session terminates before the Client reconnects, the Server MUST NOT send the Application Message to any other subscribed Client [MQTT-4.8.2-5]

If a Client responds with a PUBACK or PUBREC containing a Reason Code of 0x80 or greater to a PUBLISH packet from the Server, the Server MUST discard the Application Message and not attempt to send it to any other Subscriber [MQTT-4.8.2-6]
prohibited: The ShareName MUST NOT contain the characters "/", "+" or "#"
If the Client's Session terminates before the Client reconnects, the Server MUST NOT send the Application Message to any other subscribed Client [MQTT-4.8.2-5]

If a Client responds with a PUBACK or PUBREC containing a Reason Code of 0x80 or greater to a PUBLISH packet from the Server, the Server MUST discard the Application Message and not attempt to send it to any other Subscriber [MQTT-4.8.2-6]
recommended: If the Server is in the process of sending a QoS 1 message to its chosen subscribing Client and the connection to that Client breaks before the Server has received an acknowledgement from the Client, the Server MAY wait for the Client to reconnect and retransmit the message to that Client. If the Client's Session terminates before the Client reconnects, the Server SHOULD send the Application Message to another Client that is subscribed to the same Shared Subscription
